paper_title: High-resolution XRF scanning and age-depth model

methods.description: Process for obtaining high-resolution Sr/Ca records from split sediment core surfaces (M1-A and M2) using X-ray fluorescence (XRF) scanning at 1-cm and 2-mm resolutions.

steps.description: The Sr/Ca records were obtained by high-resolution XRF core scanning at split core surfaces in 1-cm and 2-mm resolutions.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform high-resolution XRF core scanning on sediment cores at specified resolutions to obtain Sr/Ca records.

methods.description: Comparing the obtained Sr/Ca data and ratios between the two investigated cores (M1-A and M2) to confirm robustness of our record and to exclude any potential analytical artefacts.

steps.description: We compared the Sr/Ca data and ratios between the two investigated cores (M1-A and M2) to confirm robustness of our record and to exclude any potential analytical artefacts.
steps.searchable_summary: Compare Sr/Ca data and ratios between two sediment core datasets to assess robustness and identify artifacts.

methods.method_name: Age-Depth Model Construction for Core M2
methods.description: Building a geochronological age-depth model for sediment Core M2 based on multiple dating methods including a radiocarbon date, tephra layer correlations, and a correlated lithological boundary date.

steps.description: The geochronological age of Core M2 is based on one plant remain sample, which was dated by radiocarbon methodology.
steps.searchable_summary: Incorporate a radiocarbon date from a plant remain sample into the age model.
inputs: Radiocarbon date (age and depth) from Core M2 plant remain

steps.description: Additional three datums were included in the age-depth model of this core. Two of these ages are based on visible tephra layers correlated to the known volcanic eruptions previously recognized in Core M1-A 26 .
steps.searchable_summary: Incorporate two tephra layer dates correlated from another core into the age model.
inputs: Dates and depths of two tephra layers in Core M2
outputs: Two datum points for age-depth model

steps.description: The third date corresponds to a time of marine intrusion into the lake, which is marked by a sharp boundary between lithological units three and four ( Figure 2) in both cores. It was radiometrically dated in Core M1-A 26 .
steps.searchable_summary: Incorporate a date from a lithological boundary correlated from another core into the age model.
inputs: Depth of the marine intrusion boundary in Core M2
Radiometric date from Core M1-A for the boundary
outputs: One datum point for age-depth model

steps.description: The geochronological age of Core M2 is based on one plant remain sample... Additional three datums were included in the age-depth model of this core.
steps.searchable_summary: Build an age-depth model for Core M2 using the collected datum points.
inputs: Datum points (age and depth) from steps 1-3

methods.description: Calibration of individual radiocarbon dates to calendar ages using the R package rbacon.

steps.description: Individual radiocarbon dates were calibrated using the R package rbacon 33 .
steps.searchable_summary: Calibrate radiocarbon dates using the rbacon R package.

methods.description: Visual comparison of major peaks and throughs in the Sr/Ca records from Core M1-A and Core M2 (Figure 3) to assess similarity despite lower resolution in Core M2.

steps.description: Despite lower resolution of the Core M2 record it is evident that major peaks and throughs in Sr/Ca are well presented in both datasets ( Figure 3).
steps.searchable_summary: Plot and visually inspect Sr/Ca time series data from two cores.
outputs: Visual representation of Sr/Ca records (e.g., plot)

steps.description: Despite lower resolution of the Core M2 record it is evident that major peaks and throughs in Sr/Ca are well presented in both datasets ( Figure 3).
steps.searchable_summary: Identify and compare major peaks and troughs in the two Sr/Ca records.
outputs: Observation of similarity in major features (peaks, throughs)
